description: The Inventory Management module maintains a real-time, accurate view of stock across all Ethio Nordic Trading PLC warehouses. It is tightly integrated with Procurement, Warehouse Management (WMS), and Sales & Distribution to ensure that every stock-affecting transaction is reflected instantly, supporting both FIFO (First-In-First-Out) and FEFO (First-Expired-First-Out) inventory strategies critical for pharmaceutical and regulated goods distribution.

functionalScope.title: Real-Time Inventory
functionalScope.description: Live, always-current stock-on-hand per SKU, per warehouse, per batch

functionalScope.title: Stock Movements
functionalScope.description: Full transaction history of every unit received, transferred, or issued

functionalScope.title: Expiry Monitoring
functionalScope.description: Batch-level expiry tracking with early-warning alerts

functionalScope.title: Damaged Goods
functionalScope.description: Structured logging and disposition of damaged/rejected stock

detailedWorkflow.description: When goods arrive from an international supplier and customs clearance is finalized, the Warehouse Management System records a Goods Receipt Note. This event publishes a stock-in transaction to the Inventory Management module via an internal event queue.

The Inventory module consumes the stock-in event and creates a new batch record, or increments an existing batch, with quantity, unit cost, manufacture date, and expiry date. The real-time stock ledger for that SKU and warehouse is updated atomically to prevent race conditions during concurrent transactions.

A background scheduled job continuously compares current stock levels against configured minimum stock and reorder thresholds per SKU. When stock falls below the reorder point, the system automatically generates a low-stock alert and, where configured, a draft Purchase Request routed to the Procurement module.

Each batch's expiry date is monitored daily. Batches approaching expiry trigger escalating alerts to warehouse and regulatory teams. When Sales & Distribution requests stock allocation for an order, the system automatically allocates from the earliest-expiring eligible batch first unless a batch is manually overridden with justification.

When a Sales Order or internal transfer is approved, the Inventory module deducts stock from the allocated batch or batches, decrements the ledger, and records a stock-out movement with a reference back to the originating sales order or transfer request.

Warehouse staff report damaged or discrepant stock through a structured form capturing quantity, batch, reason, and supporting photos. This creates a pending adjustment request that routes to a supervisor for approval. Once approved, the ledger is corrected and the financial impact is posted to the Finance & Accounting module.

Scheduled or ad-hoc cycle counts are performed against physical stock. Variances between system and physical counts are flagged, investigated, and resolved through the same approval-gated adjustment workflow, ensuring a full audit trail of every correction.

At period-end, or on demand, the system calculates inventory valuation using the FIFO or FEFO costing method, matching outbound stock movements to the cost of the specific batches consumed and posting the resulting cost-of-goods-sold figures to the Finance & Accounting module.

Aggregated real-time inventory value, stock aging, and expiry-risk summaries are pushed to the Executive Dashboard, and detailed inventory reports are made available through the Reporting & Business Intelligence module.
